sections.title: Introduction to the Present Tense
sections.content: The present tense in French (le présent de l'indicatif) is used to describe actions that are happening now, habitual actions, and general truths. It is the most commonly used tense and is essential for basic communication.
examples: Tu manges une pomme. (You eat an apple.)
Il travaille tous les jours. (He works every day.)

sections.title: Regular -ER Verbs
sections.content: Most French verbs ending in -er follow a regular conjugation pattern. To conjugate these verbs, remove the -er ending (the infinitive suffix) to get the stem, then add the appropriate ending based on the subject pronoun.
examples: Parler (to speak): je parle, tu parles, il/elle/on parle, nous parlons, vous parlez, ils/elles parlent
Manger (to eat): je mange, tu manges, il/elle/on mange, nous mangeons, vous mangez, ils/elles mangent
Danser (to dance): je danse, tu danses, il/elle/on danse, nous dansons, vous dansez, ils/elles dansent

sections.title: Regular -IR Verbs
sections.content: Verbs ending in -ir form another group of regular verbs. The conjugation pattern is slightly different from -er verbs. Remove the -ir ending to get the stem, then add the -ir endings.
examples: Finir (to finish): je finis, tu finis, il/elle/on finit, nous finissons, vous finissez, ils/elles finissent
Choisir (to choose): je choisis, tu choisis, il/elle/on choisit, nous choisissons, vous choisissez, ils/elles choisissent

sections.title: Regular -RE Verbs
sections.content: Verbs ending in -re are also regular, though less common than -er and -ir verbs. Remove the -re ending to get the stem, then add the -re endings.
examples: Vendre (to sell): je vends, tu vends, il/elle/on vend, nous vendons, vous vendez, ils/elles vendent
Attendre (to wait): j'attends, tu attends, il/elle/on attend, nous attendons, vous attendez, ils/elles attendent

sections.title: Common Irregular Verbs
sections.content: Some verbs have irregular conjugations in the present tense. These need to be memorized individually as they do not follow the regular patterns.
examples: Être (to be): je suis, tu es, il/elle/on est, nous sommes, vous êtes, ils/elles sont
Avoir (to have): j'ai, tu as, il/elle/on a, nous avons, vous avez, ils/elles ont
Aller (to go): je vais, tu vas, il/elle/on va, nous allons, vous allez, ils/elles vont
Faire (to do/make): je fais, tu fais, il/elle/on fait, nous faisons, vous faites, ils/elles font

sections.title: Practice Exercise
sections.content: Conjugate the following verbs in the present tense for all subject pronouns: aimer, finir, vendre, être, avoir.
